title: Classify non-retryable Pi launch failures
goal: Make deterministic Pi environmental launch failures truthful and cheap by classifying missing-auth launches as operator-action-required blockers instead of retrying them as implementation failures.
acceptance: Pi missing-auth launch failure with no assistant output is classified as agent_auth_required with retryable=false and operator_action_required=true.

A classified launch failure marks the slice and run blocked after exactly one attempt, records an incident with provider/model/profile metadata and concrete fix commands, and does not dispatch later dependency layers.
Unknown or ambiguous launch failures retain the previous retry behavior and do not get the operator-action classification.
The same classification path applies to integration repair agent calls.
The fake runner end-to-end smoke path remains green.
docs/workflow-invariants.md documents the incident payload/taxonomy and the invariant that operator-class launch failures do not consume retries.
must_ask_if: The classifier would match stderr alone without checking that the worker produced no assistant output.
The implementation would add a pre-launch auth probe or auto-login behavior.
The implementation would broaden classification so an ambiguous launch error becomes non-retryable.
New run or slice status values appear necessary.
